name: Eiffel Tower
description: Iconic iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars, symbol of France and one of the most recognizable structures in the world.

name: Louvre Museum
description: World's largest art museum housing the Mona Lisa and thousands of priceless artifacts spanning 9,000 years of art history.

name: Palace of Versailles
description: Magnificent royal chateau with baroque interiors and stunning formal gardens once home to Louis XIV.

name: French Riviera
description: Glamorous stretch of Mediterranean coastline with azure waters, luxury yachts, and world-class casinos.

name: Mont Saint-Michel
description: Medieval island abbey commune perched on a rocky tidal island, one of France's most recognizable landmarks.

name: Colosseum
description: Ancient amphitheater from 70 AD that once hosted gladiatorial combat and could hold 50,000–80,000 spectators.

name: Amalfi Coast
description: Dramatic coastal drive featuring vertiginous cliffs, colorful fishing villages, and crystalline Mediterranean waters.

name: Venice Canals
description: Unique city built on 118 small islands connected by canals, famous for gondola rides and Renaissance architecture.

name: Tuscany Vineyards
description: Rolling hills dotted with cypress trees, medieval hilltowns, and world-famous wineries producing Chianti and Brunello.

name: Cinque Terre
description: Five colorful cliffside villages connected by scenic hiking trails above the Ligurian Sea.

name: Santorini
description: Iconic volcanic island with white-washed buildings, blue-domed churches, and breathtaking sunsets over the caldera.

name: Acropolis of Athens
description: Ancient citadel containing the Parthenon and other ruins of great archaeological and cultural significance.

name: Mykonos
description: Cosmopolitan Greek island famous for its windmills, whitewashed villages, vibrant nightlife, and beautiful beaches.

name: Meteora Monasteries
description: Eastern Orthodox monasteries perched atop dramatic sandstone rock pillars in central Greece.

name: Burj Khalifa
description: World's tallest building at 828m, offering panoramic views of Dubai's futuristic skyline and desert landscape.

name: Dubai Mall
description: World's largest shopping mall featuring 1,200+ stores, an aquarium, ice rink, and the Dubai Fountain.

name: Desert Safari Dubai
description: Exhilarating 4x4 dune bashing experience through Arabian desert, followed by camel riding and Bedouin camp dinner.

name: Palm Jumeirah
description: Artificial archipelago in the shape of a palm tree hosting luxury hotels, residences, and world-class restaurants.

name: Pyramids of Giza
description: Last surviving wonder of the ancient world, the Great Pyramid of Khufu and Sphinx stand on the Giza Plateau.

name: Luxor Temple
description: Massive ancient Egyptian temple complex on the east bank of the Nile, built in 1400 BCE by Amenhotep III.

name: Abu Simbel
description: Two massive rock temples built by Ramesses II featuring four enormous seated statues on the southern facade.

name: Nile River Cruise
description: Legendary cruise along the world's longest river passing ancient temples, villages, and timeless landscapes.

name: Tokyo Shibuya Crossing
description: World's busiest pedestrian crossing where up to 3,000 people cross simultaneously, an icon of modern Tokyo.

name: Mount Fuji
description: Japan's highest peak at 3,776m, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and enduring symbol of Japanese culture.

name: Kyoto Arashiyama Bamboo Grove
description: Towering stalks of green bamboo lining the paths through the famous Arashiyama district in western Kyoto.

name: Fushimi Inari Shrine
description: Thousands of iconic vermilion torii gates trail up forested Mount Inari, a sacred Shinto site since 711 AD.

name: Machu Picchu
description: 15th-century Inca citadel set high in the Andes mountains, known as the Lost City of the Incas.

name: Amazon Rainforest Peru
description: Immense jungle ecosystem teeming with wildlife, indigenous communities, and unparalleled biodiversity.

name: Times Square
description: Iconic commercial intersection in Midtown Manhattan dazzling with neon billboards, Broadway theaters, and 24/7 energy.

name: Grand Canyon
description: Steep-sided canyon carved by the Colorado River, 277 miles long and up to 18 miles wide, a geological marvel.

name: Yellowstone National Park
description: First US national park featuring geysers, hot springs, diverse wildlife including bison and wolves.

name: Golden Gate Bridge
description: Suspension bridge spanning Golden Gate strait in San Francisco Bay, an enduring symbol of the American west.

name: Bali Ubud
description: Cultural heart of Bali surrounded by rice paddies, sacred temples, traditional dance, and artisan workshops.

name: Bali Beaches
description: World-class surf beaches at Kuta, dramatic cliffside sunset at Uluwatu, and serene shores at Seminyak.

name: Borobudur Temple
description: 9th-century Mahayana Buddhist temple, the world's largest Buddhist monument and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Angkor Wat
description: World's largest religious monument, a 12th-century Khmer Hindu temple complex spread over 400 acres.

name: Phuket
description: Thailand's largest island featuring turquoise waters, vibrant nightlife, elephant sanctuaries, and stunning coves.

name: Chiang Mai
description: Northern Thailand's cultural capital known for Buddhist temples, trekking, elephant sanctuaries, and night markets.

name: Halong Bay
description: UNESCO-listed bay with 1,600+ limestone islands and islets rising from emerald waters, explored by cruise.

name: Hoi An Ancient Town
description: Exceptionally well-preserved trading port from the 15th–19th centuries, famous for lanterns, tailors, and food.

name: Maldives Overwater Bungalows
description: Private overwater bungalows perched above turquoise lagoons offering unparalleled luxury and marine experiences.

name: Serengeti National Park
description: Africa's most famous wildlife reserve, home to the annual Great Migration of millions of wildebeest and zebra.

name: Kilimanjaro
description: Africa's highest peak at 5,895m, the world's highest free-standing mountain and a bucket-list trekking challenge.

name: Cape Town
description: Stunning city beneath Table Mountain with world-class beaches, wine regions, and vibrant cultural diversity.

name: Victoria Falls
description: World's largest waterfall by combined width and height, known as 'The Smoke That Thunders' on Zambezi River.

name: Petra
description: Rose-red rock-cut city of the Nabataean Kingdom, featuring the iconic Treasury carved into cliff faces.

name: Wadi Rum
description: Vast desert wilderness of sandstone canyons and ancient petroglyphs, the setting for countless films.

name: Dead Sea
description: Salt lake bordering Jordan and Israel, the lowest point on Earth at 430m below sea level, famous for its buoyancy.

name: Barcelona Sagrada Familia
description: Gaudí's unfinished masterpiece, an extraordinary Roman Catholic basilica under construction since 1882.

name: Alhambra Palace
description: Stunning 13th-century Moorish citadel and palace complex with intricate Islamic art and gardens.

name: Ibiza
description: Party island in the Mediterranean famous for its legendary nightlife, beautiful beaches, and bohemian culture.

name: Neuschwanstein Castle
description: 19th-century Romanesque Revival castle on a hilltop in Bavaria, the inspiration for Disney's Sleeping Beauty Castle.

name: Rio de Janeiro
description: Vibrant city beneath Christ the Redeemer featuring Copacabana beach, carnival culture, and favela tours.

name: Amazon Rainforest Brazil
description: Vast tropical rainforest covering 40% of South America, home to millions of species and indigenous tribes.

name: Iguazu Falls
description: System of 275 waterfalls stretching nearly 3km on the border of Brazil and Argentina, among the world's largest.

name: Taj Mahal
description: 17th-century white marble mausoleum built by Mughal emperor Shah Jahan, a UNESCO Wonder of the World.

name: Rajasthan
description: Land of maharajas featuring Jaipur's Pink City, majestic forts, camel treks, and colorful festivals.

name: Kerala Backwaters
description: Network of interconnected canals, rivers, and lakes parallel to the Arabian Sea coast in southern India.

name: Sydney Opera House
description: Sail-shaped performing arts center on Sydney Harbour, one of the 20th century's most distinctive buildings.

name: Great Barrier Reef
description: World's largest coral reef system spanning 2,300km, home to thousands of marine species.

name: Uluru
description: Sacred sandstone monolith at the heart of Australia rising 348m, deeply significant to the Anangu people.

name: New Zealand Milford Sound
description: Dramatic fjord in Fiordland National Park with sheer cliffs, waterfalls, and rare marine wildlife.

name: Queenstown
description: Adventure capital of the world offering bungee jumping, skydiving, skiing, and scenic mountain landscapes.

name: Marrakech Medina
description: Bustling ancient medina with maze-like souks, historic mosques, ornate riads, and vibrant Jemaa el-Fna square.

name: Sahara Desert
description: World's largest hot desert stretching across North Africa, offering camel treks and sleeping under the stars.

name: Prague Old Town
description: Remarkably preserved medieval city with Gothic churches, baroque palaces, and the famous astronomical clock.

name: Northern Lights Iceland
description: Breathtaking Aurora Borealis displays visible across Iceland's dark winter skies, a bucket-list natural wonder.

name: Blue Lagoon Iceland
description: Geothermal spa in a lava field with mineral-rich milky-blue waters, one of Iceland's most visited attractions.

name: Amsterdam Canals
description: Famous network of 17th-century canals lined with narrow merchants' houses and world-class museums.

name: Kruger National Park
description: Premier African game reserve offering the Big Five safari experience across an area the size of Wales.
